executive_summary: 2026 marks the era of the 'Great Balancing'. Inflation has largely retreated to pre-2020 norms. The primary economic narrative has shifted from price suppression to managing the deflationary pressures of rapid technological integration and demographic aging.

regional_breakdown.key_risk: Labor shortages in healthcare and construction
Energy sovereignty costs and fiscal integration challenges

supply_side: Widespread AI integration leading to significant per-capita output increase
Decentralized manufacturing (3D printing/Automation) reducing shipping dependencies
demand_side: Stable purchasing power due to low unemployment and moderate price growth
Increased spending on services and longevity-related technologies

central_bank_actions.federal_reserve: Data-dependent adjustments; interest rates stabilized at historical 'neutral' levels
central_bank_actions.ecb: Implementing 'Green Quantitative Easing' to support climate transition without inflation
central_bank_actions.boj: Active yield curve management to maintain positive but low inflation expectations

training_notes: Forward-looking 2026 dataset emphasizing the deflationary impact of high-tech productivity and the stabilization of global trade.
